title: Self-Attention (Q/K/V Mechanics)
summary: Core transformer operation. Input is projected into Q, K, V via learned matrices. Dot product $QK^\top$ measures compatibility, scaled by $\sqrt{d_k}$ to prevent softmax saturation, softmax converts to probability distribution, weighted sum of V produces contextualized output.
key_insights: Q/K/V are projections that extract different aspects: Q asks, K advertises, V holds content
Dot product = alignment/correlation between query and key vectors
Scaling by $\sqrt{d_k}$ prevents large dot products from pushing softmax into near-one-hot
Softmax converts raw scores to probability distribution (rows sum to 1)
Output for each token is a weighted average of all value vectors — creates contextualized representations
Self-attention is permutation-invariant — no inherent notion of order (→ need positional encoding)

dimensions.Q_K_V: each $[B, n, d_{\text{model}}]$ after projection, then $[B, h, n, d_k]$ per head
dimensions.output: $[B, h, n, d_k]$ per head, then $[B, n, d_{\text{model}}]$ after concat

mathematical_core.complexity: $O(n^2 \cdot d_k)$ per head for score computation

derivations.title: Why 1/√d_k — Complete causal chain from unscaled dot products to training failure

steps.name: 1. Dot products grow with d_k
steps.math: $s = Q \cdot K = \sum_{i=1}^{d_k} Q_i K_i$. Each term: $\mathbb{E}[Q_i K_i] = 0$, $\text{Var}(Q_i K_i) = 1$. By independence: $\text{Var}(s) = d_k$
steps.result: Score magnitude grows as $\sqrt{d_k}$. For $d_k = 64$, std dev $\approx 8$.

steps.name: 2. Large scores → softmax saturation
steps.math: $\alpha_j = \frac{e^{s_j}}{\sum_k e^{s_k}}$. When $\text{Var}(s)$ is large, one $s_{j^*} \gg$ others with high probability.
steps.result: $\alpha_{j^*} \approx 1$, all other $\alpha_k \approx 0$. Softmax becomes near one-hot.

steps.name: 3. Saturation → vanishing gradients through softmax
steps.math: $\frac{\partial \alpha_j}{\partial s_l} = \alpha_j(\delta_{jl} - \alpha_l)$. In saturated regime: $\alpha_{j^*} \approx 1$, all others $\approx 0$. All four cases give $\frac{\partial \alpha}{\partial s} \approx 0$.
steps.result: ALL partial derivatives $\approx 0$. Gradients through softmax vanish completely.

steps.name: 4. Attention degenerates to hard lookup
steps.result: No nuanced mixing. Cannot adjust which key to attend to (gradients dead). Brittle discrete lookup.

steps.name: 5. Compounds across layers
steps.math: $J_{\text{total}} = J_L \cdot J_{L-1} \cdots J_1$. Each $J$ has near-zero entries from saturated softmax.
steps.result: Product of near-zero Jacobians → classic vanishing gradient across depth.

steps.name: 6. Scaling fixes everything
steps.result: Scores in moderate range, softmax stays soft, gradients flow, attention remains expressive.

derivations.summary: $1/\sqrt{d_k}$ is a variance-normalizing, gradient-preserving, expressivity-protecting temperature control.
derivations.connects_to: Xavier/Kaiming initialization — same philosophy of variance control at each layer

derivations.title: Softmax Jacobian structure
derivations.math:
$$\frac{\partial \alpha_j}{\partial s_l} = \alpha_j(\delta_{jl} - \alpha_l)$$
This is a rank-1 perturbation of a diagonal matrix: $J = \text{diag}(\alpha) - \alpha \alpha^\top$
derivations.consequence: Eigenvalues bounded by $\max(\alpha_i)(1 - \max(\alpha_i))$. Maximum gradient flow when attention is uniform ($\alpha = 1/n$). Minimum when peaked.

worked_examples.title: 4-token attention computation
worked_examples.note: Full numerical walkthrough with Q,K matrices, raw scores, scaling, masking, softmax, value retrieval

geometric_properties: Q and K vectors should be well-distributed in $d_k$ space — collapse means uniform (useless) attention
Monitor average cosine similarity between Q vectors per head
Output vectors should not all converge (representation collapse)
After scaling, score distribution should have variance $\approx 1$ — verify as diagnostic
statistical_properties: Attention entropy $H = -\sum_{ij} A_{ij} \log A_{ij}$ — high = uniform, low = peaked
Very low entropy may indicate saturation even with scaling
Monitor $\|Q\|, \|K\|$ norm distributions — varying norms cause attention dominated by high-norm positions
numerical_properties: Softmax stability: subtract $\max(S_{\text{row}})$ before $\exp()$ to prevent overflow
Without scaling at $d_k = 64$: score std $\approx 8$, softmax inputs in $[-24, +24]$ — deep saturation

prerequisites: Variance of sums of random variables
related: TRANSFORMER.POSITIONAL_ENCODING — needed because self-attention is permutation-invariant
Xavier/Kaiming initialization — same variance control philosophy
Softmax temperature — $1/\sqrt{d_k}$ is fixed temperature; could be learned

notes: Scaling derivation initially missed in assessment — understood after guided exploration
Future: connection to Xavier/Kaiming init, learned temperature parameters
